behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: model-judgment seam (vision classifier output quality) plus decision handoff. A stubbed classifier would hide real false negatives on explicit imagery, so real model behavior is required. Rejected Workflow: behavior text is a single image-classification decision ('An image ... is flagged'), not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run a moderation test with real vision-model inference on a curated explicit-image set through the moderation service, and assert each submission is routed to hold-for-review and never reaches publish state.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: model-judgment seam for robust hate-speech detection under adversarial obfuscation. Deterministic mocks will pass routing but cannot expose real model robustness gaps. Rejected Workflow: this is one model judgment requirement ('text post ... is flagged'), not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Send obfuscated hate-speech variants (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, mixed unicode) to the real text moderation model via the service and verify each is flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between image-check result, text-check result, and publish/hold state transition. Failure is in local module wiring/state propagation (including 'silently dropped'), which can be caught with in-process fakes. Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no dependence on real AI output quality because inputs are predefined as pass, (c) no multi-agentic chain required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator, repositories/queues, and publisher with deterministic pass responses from both classifiers; assert final persisted state is published and that no hold/drop path is taken.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: multi-step orchestration seam with sequential agentic calls and end-to-end latency SLO. A lower-level test with stubs can miss real combined latency and orchestration timing failures. Rejected System: behavior text explicitly requires the full chain ('image check, text check, publish decision'), which is a multi-step agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through the real moderation stack using real image and text model calls, measure submit-to-decision latency, and assert p95 (or required percentile) stays <=5s under representative peak-hour load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (external model availability/network failure) plus retry-queue handoff. Real outage modes (timeouts, connection failures, 5xx patterns) are often hidden by simple mocks; this behavior is about true unavailability handling. Rejected Agentic: correctness here is failure-path control flow, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-service unavailability (e.g., unreachable endpoint or controlled 5xx/timeout in a live test environment) and verify image posts are persisted into retry queue and never transition to approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: model-judgment seam under repeated borderline inference, potentially exposing nondeterminism/calibration drift. Mocked responses force consistency and can hide real instability. Rejected Workflow: while repeated submissions are multiple calls, behavior text targets consistency of one judgment type (borderline image decision), not a full multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the defined short window against the real vision moderation path and assert decision consistency rate meets policy threshold (e.g., no conflicting hold/publish outcomes beyond allowed tolerance).
